data.description:
## Reproduction Steps

1. AWS account 896586841071, us-east-1: inspect the `voter_errors` metric in CloudWatch namespace `rebar/host` (instance i-00880b2c7f13527c5) over 2026-07-05..07.
2. Review the `rebar-gerrit-voter-errors` alarm history (`aws cloudwatch describe-alarm-history --alarm-name rebar-gerrit-voter-errors`).

## Expected vs Actual

- Expected: the review-bot (the 'voter') casts the Gerrit LLM-Review vote with zero errors; `voter_errors` stays at 0.
- Actual: 52 `voter_errors` in recurring bursts across 07-05 17:27 PT -> 07-06 21:27 PT (2-5/period, peak Sum=10 in the 09:27 PT hour). The `rebar-gerrit-voter-errors` alarm (fires on any datapoint>0) flapped OK<->ALARM repeatedly on 07-06 evening; last recovered ALARM->OK at 07-06 22:17 PT. Clean and OK for ~19h since.
- Impact: the voter casts the Gerrit LLM-Review gate vote — intermittent failures risk stalling the review gate (currently recovered).

## Investigation blocker

The Gerrit host ships NO logs to CloudWatch Logs (only snap-demo groups exist), so the voter-service error text lives only in the EC2 instance journald and is not retrievable read-only via CloudWatch. Root-causing needs on-host access.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Identify the voter/review-bot failure cause from the host's journald (voter service logs) for the 07-05/06 window.
- [ ] Decide whether to ship voter/review-bot logs to a CloudWatch log group so future occurrences are diagnosable remotely.
- [ ] Confirm no LLM-Review votes were actually dropped during the window (or re-cast any that were).
data.title: Gerrit review-bot voter errors: 52 intermittent LLM-Review voting failures over 48h (2026-07-05/06)
